author: y4rr
content:
Phase 3 complete. Created ferrotorch-ml workspace crate (~700 LOC, 27 tests).

Workspace member added at ferrotorch-ml/. Cargo.toml depends on:
- ferrotorch-core (Tensor type)
- ndarray = 0.17 (matches ferrolearn / ferray)
- ferrolearn-core, -metrics, -preprocess, -model-sel, -decomp, -datasets (all 0.3.0, published on crates.io)

Module layout:
- adapter: Tensor <-> ndarray::{Array1, Array2} round-trip primitives. tensor_to_array1, tensor_to_array2, array1_to_tensor, array2_to_tensor, plus tensor_to_array1_usize / array1_usize_to_tensor for sklearn label paths. Every fn calls require_cpu() at entry; GPU input returns NotImplementedOnCuda.
- metrics: 7 regression metrics (r2_score, MSE, RMSE, MAE, median_AE, max_error, explained_variance) + accuracy_score classification. Each takes &Tensor<T> and routes through the adapter automatically. Trait bound: T: Float + num_traits::Float + Send + Sync + 'static.
- datasets: 5 generators (make_classification, make_regression with n_informative, make_blobs with cluster_std, make_moons, make_circles) + 3 toy loaders (load_iris, load_wine, load_breast_cancer). All return (Tensor<F>, Tensor<F>) — the X feature matrix and y label/target vector. Class labels are encoded as floats so they round-trip through the adapter.
- preprocess / decomposition / model_selection: direct re-export modules of ferrolearn-{preprocess, decomp, model-sel}. Users get the full sklearn surface (StandardScaler, MinMaxScaler, PCA, FastICA, t-SNE, UMAP, KFold, GridSearchCV, train_test_split, ...) one import away. Drive these from Tensor data via the adapter.

GPU discipline (apply /rust-gpu-discipline before claiming done):
- Crate-level docstring documents the CPU-only design choice explicitly.
- adapter::require_cpu is the sole gate; every public adapter / metric / dataset entry routes through it before touching ndarray.
- No silent .to(Device::Cpu) anywhere — that would obscure GPU->CPU transfer cost. Verified via grep.
- README documents the explicit user pattern: 
- Forbidden patterns clean.

Tests (27 total):
- adapter (8): array1 / array2 round-trip, transposed (non-contiguous) Array2 handling, shape-mismatch rejection, array1_usize round-trip + negative + NaN rejection
- metrics (8): R^2 perfect-prediction (=1) + constant-baseline (=0), MSE/RMSE/MAE/max_error closed forms, accuracy_score perfect + partial, CPU-tensor smoke
- datasets (10): every generator + loader checks shape; integer-label range checks for classification outputs; iris_self_classify_is_perfect_accuracy verifies the dataset → metric end-to-end path

Implementation notes:
- ferrolearn's metrics signatures use  (not a custom FerroFloat trait); my wrappers match that bound.
- make_blobs and make_regression have extra args vs the audit-doc skeleton (cluster_std, n_informative); aligned to the actual published 0.3.0 signatures.
- ferrolearn-core needed an explicit dep (the FerroError type isn't re-exported by the metrics/datasets crates).

Audit doc docs/audit/README.md updated with row 17 marking Phase 3 done. Closes #571.

Workspace tests: 4593 passed, 0 failed, 100 ignored (was 4566; +27 new ferrotorch-ml tests).

Follow-ups filed (kept open):
- #598 extend metrics coverage (precision, recall, F1, ROC-AUC, log_loss, confusion_matrix, ranking, clustering)
- #599 2-D scoring metrics (log_loss/brier take Array2 prob outputs — needs an extra adapter)
